channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Harris slammed for pledging millions to Lebanon as North Carolina suffers
item.description: Users on social media lashed out at Vice President Harris after her post on X pledged millions of aid for Lebanon despite the ongoing hurricane recovery in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump supporter Elon Musk offers massive hourly pay to those working to increase voter turnout
item.description: Elon Musk's pro-Trump super PAC, America, is offering a $30 hourly base salary to canvassers in battleground states working to increase voter registration and turnout.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Walz says he'll 'own up' when he misspeaks as the Democratic ticket steps up media interviews
item.description: The “Fox News Sunday” interview reflected a broader media blitz by presidential nominee Kamala Harris and Walz as the Democrats seek to garner public attention in the final 30 days of the campaign.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk makes his first appearance at a Trump rally and casts the election in dire terms
item.description: Billionaire tech executive Elon Musk cast the upcoming presidential election in dire terms during an appearance with Donald Trump, calling the Republican presidential nominee the only candidate “to preserve democracy in America.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands join pro-Palestinian rallies around the globe as Oct. 7 anniversary nears
item.description: Police used tear gas and water cannons to disperse violent demonstrators in Rome as tens of thousands pro-Palestinian protesters took to the streets in European cities and around the globe, calling for a cease-fire as the first anniversary of the Hamas attacks on Israel approached.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: KJP slammed after Hurricane Helene over mixed messages on whether FEMA resources used for migrants
item.description: White House press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre is under fire for apparently contradicting herself regarding the use of FEMA funds for illegal immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Absentee voting kicks off in Maine
item.description: Maine joins a growing majority of U.S. states that have kicked off the 2024 election with early voting. It is one of two states in the U.S. that does not use a winner-take-all system.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Photos: A visual look at Trump's return to Pennsylvania for a rally at site of assassination attempt
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Thousands of supporters returned to Butler, Pennsylvania, on Saturday to rally around Donald Trump at the same site where a gunman tried to assassinate him in July. Many had lined up as the sun rose for the event billed as a “tribute to the American spirit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump returns to rally crowd in Butler, Pa., site of July assassination attempt
item.description: The former President spoke at a campaign rally in the same location that a bullet grazed his ear in July. The shooter killed a man and injured two other people.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman, MPR News

item.title: How the Butler shooting changed Donald Trump's campaign
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump was onstage at a rally on Long Island last month, talking about taxes, when he appeared momentarily spooked by something he had spotted over his shoulder. “I thought this was a wise guy coming up,” he explained, joking that he was getting his elbow ready to fight back.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden pledged to campaign hard for Harris. So far, he's been mostly a no-show
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — On the last day of August, President Joe Biden was asked about his fall campaign plans. He promised a Labor Day appearance in Pittsburgh and said he would be “on the road from there on.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and BILL BARROW, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Major cases before the Supreme Court deal with transgender rights, guns, nuclear waste and vapes
item.description: The Supreme Court's new term begins Monday with a handful of important cases set to be heard and the possibility that the justices will be asked to get involved in election disputes.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In Michigan, Harris doesn't get hoped-for firefighters endorsement amid shifting labor loyalties
item.description: REDFORD TOWNSHIP, Mich. (AP) — It was the perfect place to welcome the endorsement of the firefighters union — a gleaming new firehouse in a blue-collar town just outside of Detroit in the key battleground state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Civil rights groups ask to extend voter registration deadlines in hurricane-ravaged states
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — With registration deadlines looming, Democrats and civil rights groups are asking election officials in the states ravaged by Hurricane Helene to give voters more time. A judge in South Carolina on Friday extended that state's deadline to Oct.
item.dc:creator: AYANNA ALEXANDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Retired New Jersey State Police trooper who stormed Capitol is sentenced to probation
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A retired New Jersey State Police trooper who stormed the U.S. Capitol with a mob of Donald Trump supporters was sentenced to probation instead of prison on Friday, as the federal courts reached a milestone in the punishment of Capitol rioters.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Obama to campaign for Harris in key swing states, starting with Pennsylvania
item.description: Obama’s first campaign stop for Harris will be in the Pittsburgh area on Oct. 10, according to a senior campaign official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, Kemp make rare joint appearance in Helene aftermath
item.description: Former President Trump and Georgia Gov. Brian Kemp (R) on Friday made their first public appearance together in years, putting aside simmering tensions to highlight how Hurricane Helene has devastated the state.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden says he is worried about violence around the presidential election
item.description: President Biden told reporters he was confident the election “will be free and fair,” before saying, “I don’t know whether it will be peaceful.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith, MPR News

item.title: US arranges flights to bring Americans out of Lebanon as others seek escape
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S.-arranged flights have brought about 350 Americans and their immediate relatives out of Lebanon this week during escalated fighting between Israel and Hezbollah, while thousands of others still there face airstrikes and diminishing commercial flights.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER, MATTHEW LEE and JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs arrested during traffic stop: NYPD
item.description: EAST HARLEM, Manhattan (PIX11) – New York state Assemblyman Eddie Gibbs was arrested during a traffic stop in Manhattan on Thursday, according to the NYPD. The East Harlem Democrat was taken into custody in front of 1844 Lexington Ave. around 1:30 p.m., police said.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Volunteers open Harris-Walz campaign office in Gary
item.description: The Harris-Walz campaign on Thursday opened Indiana's first and only Democratic presidential campaign headquarters in Gary, celebrated with a "Kamala Karavan" of vehicles adorned with campaign decor.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Barnes, WGN 9

item.title: Firefighters union won't endorse in Trump-Harris race
item.description: The International Association of Fire Fighters (IAFF) announced Thursday it would not endorse a candidate in this year's presidential race, becoming the second major union to do so in recent weeks.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Helene renews calls for Congress to pass disaster funding
item.description: The widespread devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene is upping the pressure on lawmakers for a looming end-of-year fight over disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Programming alert: NewsNation to feature PA Senate race tonight
item.description: NewsNation will have live coverage of the Pennsylvania senate debate between Bob Casey and David McCormick.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Voters in 13 states have less than a week to register. When is the deadline in Illinois?
item.description: The 2024 presidential election is fast approaching, and in some states, the voter registration deadline is as soon as this week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: Valadao, Salas spar over issues in California debate
item.description: David Valadao and Rudy Salas debated over issues voters said are of the most importance to them during a debate.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, NewsNation

item.title: Trump on Springfield Haitian migrants: 'They have to be removed'
item.description: The former president spoke to NewsNation's Ali Bradley about his plans regarding the Haitian migrants living in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Marquette poll has Harris over Trump in Wisconsin, but within margin of error
item.description: Marquette University professor Charles Franklin joined the WGN Evening News on Wednesday to break down the latest numbers from the law school's poll about the upcoming presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ray Cortopassi, WGN 9

item.title: ACLU of Nevada attempts to stop purge of nearly 20,000 registered voters
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- The ACLU of Nevada intervened in a court action Wednesday, which attempts to stop the purging of nearly 20,000 registered voters weeks before the presidential election. On Sept.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Minneapolis grants consent for Blue Line extension through city’s north side
item.description: The Minneapolis City Council signed off Wednesday on the proposed route of a light rail line that will run through a section of the city’s north side. However, some council members said they could only get to a yes vote after some of their concerns with the route were addressed.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota abortion law signed by Walz scrutinized during VP debate
item.description: Internet searches about Minnesota’s abortion law — the first state to codify access after the Supreme Court overturned Roe v. Wade — were high during Tuesday night’s vice presidential debate.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: State of Democra-Z: Takeaways from the vice presidential debate
item.description: The vice presidential debate is not expected to push many voters to one side or another, but it offered a closer look at the men who could be second in command. We check in with two Gen-Z voters about what stood out to them.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Trump rejects calls for another debate with Harris after VP showdown
item.description: Former President Donald Trump again rejected the prospect of another debate with Vice President Kamala Harris late Tuesday following a showdown between the two vice presidential nominees, saying he was "not looking to do it again.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Volunteers from deep blue Illinois learn to canvas in battleground states
item.description: Across the seven states likely to decide the presidential election, thousands of volunteers, including some from Illinois, are on the ground going door-to-door working to increase voter turnout.
item.dc:creator: Tahman Bradley, WGN 9

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump suggests ‘1 rough hour’ of policing would end retail crime
item.description: The former president called for law enforcement to be freed up to "do their job" without restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
